rationale.R1.1: Understanding the contribution claim (self-supervised tool-augmented ASR) is a prerequisite for any meaningful opinion; the framing as 'tool-learning' vs. standard lexicon-constrained decoding must be reconstructed to evaluate anything else.
rationale.R1.2: There is a visible tension between the narrative ('model learns when to invoke tools') and the technical description (hard WFST constraint + fixed OOV penalty of -8.0), making logical consistency a central concern regardless of what 'what do you think' ultimately targets.
rationale.R1.3: The headline claims (WER gains, 'self-supervised tool selection') need to be checked against the reported experimental setup; only two baselines are mentioned, one of which is unconstrained CTC rather than a comparable lexicon-constrained system.
rationale.R1.4: Any general opinion on a research artifact naturally implicates novelty—whether the delta over standard WFST-constrained CTC is real or repackaged is load-bearing for a positive vs. skeptical verdict.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a critical technical assessment of whether the method's claims (novelty, logical coherence, evidence) hold up, or a writing-quality review of how clearly and compellingly the work is presented?
